question: According to the four basic principles of wound care detailed on Slide 3, what specific clinical action is mandated if a wound is evaluated as being "too dry"?
options: It will require aggressive absolute absorption using specialized hydrofibres
It will need hydration
It requires immediate debridement utilizing a Debrisoft lolly
It must be packed with a dry alginate sheet until a gel forms naturally

question: If a wound produces excessive exudates (wound fluid), what basic principle of wound care must be applied?
options: This needs to be absorbed
The wound must be hydrated via hydrogels immediately
An interactive silicone scar protectant spray should be applied
The clinician should treat it with a basic layer of dry Jelonet gauze • • • • Tissue Viability Academy • Exam Paper 7 of 20 Page 1 of 8

question: The basic principles state that if a wound has necrotic (dead) tissue or slough (yellow debris), what explicit action is required?
options: Application of a liquid film protectant layer to lock in moisture
It will need debridement
Immediate coverage using high-flow iodine ointments exclusively
The termination of all clinical cleaning and irrigation solutions

question: Under the 4 basic principles, if a wound is identified as being actively infected, what clinical intervention is required?
options: Hydration using a non-sting incontinence cleanser foam spray
It needs to be treated with the appropriate antibacterial agent
Immediate surgical containment via primary intention adhesive tape strips
Closure of the tissue using specialized scar silicone management sheets

question: To accurately answer questions regarding a wound's hydration, exudate, slough, or infection status, what must a nurse complete first?
options: A local box stock count inside the central dressing cupboard
A full holistic wound assessment using T.I.M.E.R.S and/or T.I.L.I.
An independent telephone log query directly to a regional pharmacy board
A manual script translation of the Greater Manchester formulary index codes
